title: Pediatric eczema follow-up
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.
assessment.rationale: The exact UAE DHA history section supports documenting pediatric eczema interval course, itch, dryness, symptoms, sleep or functional impact, morphology and age-linked distribution without inferring severity or cause.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis
source.population: Children and adults with atopic dermatitis complaints.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth atopic-dermatitis documentation; partial for every eczema or contact-dermatitis subtype.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

assessment.text: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.
assessment.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports documenting rapid worsening, pain, fever, lethargy, blistering, infection, mucosal or systemic concern only when assessed; it does not assert absence or generate escalation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis
source.population: Children and adults with atopic dermatitis complaints.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth atopic-dermatitis documentation; partial for every eczema or contact-dermatitis subtype.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

assessment.text: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.
assessment.rationale: The management section supports documenting a clinician-entered plan, safety net, arranged follow-up and parent or patient questions without generating skin-care, medicine, dose, referral, or emergency advice.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis
source.population: Children and adults with atopic dermatitis complaints.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth atopic-dermatitis documentation; partial for every eczema or contact-dermatitis subtype.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

assessment.text: Supports morphology and distribution review, atopy history, dry skin, selected non-routine tests, and differential-diagnosis assessment.
assessment.rationale: Morphology, age-linked distribution, atopy, dry-skin, differential, and selected investigation context were reviewed; no diagnosis or test is generated.
